name: Bhai Darshan Singh Kamal (Delhi)
body: Bhai Darshan Singh Ji Kamal was born on the 14th of August, 1934 to Mata Hukam Kaur Ji and Sardar Phuman Singh Ji. They belonged to the village of Naroor Panshta in Phagwara, Punjab. Kamal Ji grew up in this village with 3 brothers and 3 sisters. When he was very young, he came down with chicken pox and this affected his eyes. As the hospital was far from their home, his parents tried some home remedies but unfortunately, he lost his eyesight at the age of 4.

He was sent to learn Kirtan from his namesake, Prof. Darshan Singh Komal of Nangal Khurd, Hoshiarpur. Prof. Darshan Singh Komal was custodian to the knowledge and tradition of Kirtan that was passed down to him by his teacher, the renowned Rababi of Amritsar, Bhai Sain Ditta Ji. Prof. Darshan Singh Komal trained thousands of Kirtanis throughout his years of teaching. Some of his notable students include Bhai Dharam Singh and Bhai Shamsher Singh Zakhmi, Bhai Didar Singh, Bhai Beant Singh Bijli, Bhai Sital Singh Sitara, Bhai Gian Singh Surjit and many others. Bhai Darshan Singh Kamal spent between 5 to 7 years learning from Komal Ji and at the end of his training, Prof. Darshan Singh Komal gifted him with the  title ‘Kamal’ to be used at the end of his name. Komal Ji recommended him to go to Putli Ghar, Amritsar and during his time there, he was in the company of Bhai Gopal Singh Ji.

He encountered many challenges due to being visually impaired. He used to carry his harmonium and travel on his own from one village to another for Kirtan programs. It was difficult for him, so he came to Delhi in 1957 and completed his masters degree in music. During his final examination, he played the violin and aced his test. He began his career as a high school music teacher in Delhi. In 1971, he came under the employment of the Delhi Sikh Gurdwara Management Committee and served as a Hazoori Raagi in Gurdwara Sees Ganj Sahib for 20 years.

He made his first tour abroad to Tehran, Iran. His next tour was to the United Kingdom in 1974 followed by various other tours around the world including Singapore, Kuwait, America, Canada and Mexico. The late Harbhajan Singh Yogi Ji was very impressed with his Kirtan and invited him for Kirtan many times. He also helped to get Kamal Ji’s family settled in the United States. His long time ‘saathi’ for 20 years on tabla was Bhai Mastan Singh Ji. Some of his other ‘saathis’ on the side harmonium include Bhai Pritam Singh Ji and Bhai Nirmal Singh Ji. Bhai Darshan Singh Kamal passed away peacefully on 23rd March 2020. The information above was obtained from Bhai Darshan Singh Kamal’s son. If anyone has more recordings of Bhai Darshan Singh Kamal to share, please contact us.
